title: Calvin Can’t Fly
author: Jennifer Berne
description: A heartwarming story about a starling named Calvin who loves to read books instead of learning how to fly, only to find that his knowledge saves his entire family from a dangerous storm.

sentences.englishOriginal: Welcome to Storyline Online.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歡迎來到 Storyline Online。

sentences.englishOriginal: Brought to you by the SAG-AFTRA Foundation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 本節目由 SAG-AFTRA 基金會為您呈現。

phraseNotes.title: brought to you by
phraseNotes.explanation: 「brought to you by」是節目、廣播或活動中常見的固定片語，意思是「由……贊助/提供/呈現」。

examples.english: This podcast is brought to you by our generous listeners.
examples.chinese: 本播客節目是由我們慷慨的聽眾贊助播出的。

grammarNotes.title: Past Participle Clause (減縮子句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 這裡的「Brought to you by...」是「This show is brought to you by...」的省略用法，利用過去分詞（PP）引導的片語來作補充說明。

examples.english: Made in Taiwan, this bicycle is of excellent quality.
examples.chinese: 這輛腳踏車是台灣製造的，品質非常優良。

sentences.englishOriginal: I’m Jonathan Pryce, and today I’m going to read Calvin Can’t Fly, written by Jennifer Berne and illustrated by Keith Bendis.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是強納森·普萊斯，今天我要讀《凱文不會飛》給大家聽，這本書由珍妮佛·伯恩所著，基斯·班迪斯繪製插圖。

vocabularyNotes.title: illustrate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指為書本、雜誌等「繪製插圖」或「加插圖於」。

examples.english: She was hired to illustrate a new children's book.
examples.chinese: 她被雇用為一本新的童書繪製插圖。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin is a starling.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文是一隻歐洲椋鳥。

vocabularyNotes.title: starling
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「椋鳥」，一種常見的小型鳥類，常群體行動。

examples.english: A large flock of starlings flew across the evening sky.
examples.chinese: 一大群椋鳥飛過了傍晚的天空。

sentences.englishOriginal: He was born under the eaves of an old barn with his three brothers, four sisters, and sixty-seven thousand four hundred and thirty-two cousins.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他出生在一座舊穀倉的屋簷下，有三個哥哥弟弟、四個姐姐妹妹，還有六萬七千四百三十二個堂表兄弟姊妹。

vocabularyNotes.title: eaves
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞（常用複數），指房屋的「屋簷」。

examples.english: Swallows built a nest under the eaves of the house.
examples.chinese: 燕子在房子的屋簷下築了一個巢。

sentences.englishOriginal: Starlings have BIG families.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 椋鳥的家族可是非常龐大的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Right from the beginning Calvin was different . . . right from the day all the little starlings tumbled out of their nests to discover the world.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 從一開始，凱文就與眾不同……就從所有小椋鳥從鳥巢裡跌跌撞撞地爬出來去探索世界的那一天起。

vocabularyNotes.title: tumble
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「跌倒」、「滾落」或「跌跌撞撞地走」。這裡用來形容小鳥從巢中爬出時笨拙可愛的模樣。

examples.english: The puppies tumbled over each other in the grass.
examples.chinese: 小狗們在草地上互相滾來滾去地玩耍。

phraseNotes.title: right from the beginning
phraseNotes.explanation: 固定片語，強調「一開始就……」或「打從一開始」。

examples.english: I knew right from the beginning that this project would be a success.
examples.chinese: 我打從一開始就知道這個專案會成功。

sentences.englishOriginal: Charlie discovered worms.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 查理發現了蠕蟲。

sentences.englishOriginal: Adeline discovered grass.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 阿德琳發現了草地。

sentences.englishOriginal: Clement discovered dirt.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 克萊門特發現了泥土。

sentences.englishOriginal: Aubrey discovered water.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 奧布里發現了水。

sentences.englishOriginal: And Calvin . . . well, Calvin discovered a BOOK.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而凱文呢……嗯，凱文發現了一本書。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Well, ...
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 在口語或故事敘述中，用「Well」放在句首，用來做語氣的轉折或停頓，類似中文的「嗯……」、「這個嘛……」，能帶出令人意想不到的結果。

examples.english: I thought he would be angry. Well, he actually laughed.
examples.chinese: 我以為他會生氣。嗯，結果他居然笑了。

sentences.englishOriginal: While all of Calvin’s cousins chased beetles, bugs, and ants, Calvin was learning to read letters, words, and sentences.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當凱文的所有堂表兄弟姊妹都在追逐甲蟲、小蟲和螞蟻時，凱文卻在學習認字母、單字和句子。

grammarNotes.title: Past Continuous Tense (過去進行式)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「was learning」使用過去進行式，用來描繪過去某一段時間內正在持續發生的動作，在此處與 cousins 的習慣形成強烈對比。

examples.english: While they were playing video games, she was studying for the exam.
examples.chinese: 當他們在玩電玩時，她正在為考試讀書。

sentences.englishOriginal: At night, his cousins dreamed of insect eating and garbage picking.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 到了晚上，他的親戚們夢想著吃昆蟲和撿垃圾。

phraseNotes.title: dream of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意思是「夢見」或「夢想著某事」。後面常接名詞或動名詞（V-ing）。

examples.english: She always dreams of traveling around the world.
examples.chinese: 她總是夢想著能環遊世界。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin dreamed of adventure stories, legends, and poetry.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文則是夢見了冒險故事、傳奇傳說和詩歌。

vocabularyNotes.title: legend
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「傳說」或「傳奇故事」。

examples.english: The book is full of ancient legends about dragons.
examples.chinese: 這本書充滿了關於龍的古代傳說。

sentences.englishOriginal: He even dreamed that someday he’d become a great writer himself.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他甚至夢想著有一天自己能成為一名偉大的作家。

sentences.englishOriginal: On June first, when all the little starlings lined up for flying lessons with their teacher, Mr. Wingstead, Calvin was nowhere to be seen.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 六月一日這天，當所有小椋鳥都排好隊，準備跟著他們的老師溫斯泰德先生上飛行課時，凱文卻不見蹤影。

phraseNotes.title: line up
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，意思是「排隊」或「排列成行」。

examples.english: The students lined up in alphabetical order.
examples.chinese: 學生們按照字母順序排好隊。

phraseNotes.title: nowhere to be seen
phraseNotes.explanation: 固定片語，字面意思是「哪裡都看不到」，用來生動地形容某人或某物「完全找不到」、「不見蹤影」。

examples.english: When the bill arrived, he was nowhere to be seen.
examples.chinese: 買單的時候，他的人影完全不見了。

sentences.englishOriginal: “Where is that bird?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「那隻鳥到底在哪裡？」

sentences.englishOriginal: Actually, Calvin was in the library.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 事實上，凱文人在圖書館裡。

sentences.englishOriginal: As the other little starlings were learning to SWOOP and HOVER and FLY figure eights, Calvin buried his beak in books.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當其他小椋鳥正在學習「俯衝」、「盤旋」和「飛八字形」時，凱文卻把他的鳥嘴埋進了書堆裡。

vocabularyNotes.title: swoop
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指鳥類或飛機「猛撲」或「向下俯衝」。

examples.english: The eagle swooped down to catch the fish.
examples.chinese: 老鷹向下俯衝去抓那條魚。

vocabularyNotes.title: hover
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指鳥類或直升機在空中「盤旋」或「翱翔停滯」。

examples.english: A hummingbird can hover in mid-air.
examples.chinese: 蜂鳥能夠在半空中盤旋。

phraseNotes.title: bury one's beak/head in books
phraseNotes.explanation: 延伸自「bury one's head in books」的生動說法。字面意思是把頭（鳥嘴）埋在書裡，用來形容一個人「埋頭苦讀」或「沉浸在書本中」。

examples.english: Instead of going to the party, he spent the weekend burying his head in books.
examples.chinese: 他沒有去參加派對，而是整個週末都在埋頭苦讀。

sentences.englishOriginal: And there his mind soared.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在那裡，他的思想盡情翱翔。

vocabularyNotes.title: soar
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指鳥類「高飛」、「翱翔」，這裡抽象地指凱文的心靈或思想「飛躍」、「自由馳騁」。

examples.english: Reading allowed her imagination to soar.
examples.chinese: 閱讀讓她的想像力自由翱翔。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin read about pirates and cavemen . . . Volcanoes and rainbows . . . Whales and dinosaurs . . . Calvin especially loved dinosaurs!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文讀到了海盜和原始人……火山和彩虹……鯨魚和恐龍……凱文特別喜歡恐龍！

sentences.englishOriginal: He learned how the planets circle the sun, what makes the wind blow, and amazing facts, like how caterpillars grow up to become butterflies.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他了解了行星是如何圍繞太陽運行、風是怎麼吹起來的，以及一些神奇的事實，比如毛毛蟲長大後是如何變成蝴蝶的。

vocabularyNotes.title: caterpillar
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「毛毛蟲」。

examples.english: The caterpillar crawled slowly along the green leaf.
examples.chinese: 毛毛蟲在綠葉上緩慢地爬行。

sentences.englishOriginal: His books took him to places wings never could.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他的書帶他去了翅膀永遠無法到達的地方。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: take someone to places
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 這是一個非常優美的英文表達，字面意思是「帶某人去某些地方」，常用來比喻某種嗜好、知識或才華開闊了某人的視野，帶給他們無限的體驗。

examples.english: Her talent for music took her to places she had never dreamed of.
examples.chinese: 她的音樂才華帶她去了許多她做夢也沒想過的地方。

sentences.englishOriginal: And his heart fluttered with excitement.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他的心因為興奮而怦怦直跳。

vocabularyNotes.title: flutter
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指鳥類翅膀「振翅」或「拍動」，用在心臟時形容「（因興奮或緊張而）怦怦跳、心頭小鹿亂撞」。

examples.english: My heart fluttered when he looked at me.
examples.chinese: 當他看著我時，我的心漏跳了一拍。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin’s cousins called him “nerdy birdie,” “geeky beaky,” and “bookworm.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文的堂表兄弟姊妹叫他「書呆子鳥」、「怪咖大嘴巴」和「書蟲」。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: nerdy birdie / geeky beaky
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 這是英文童書中常見的押韻取綽號方式（Rhyming slang）。nerdy（書呆子）搭配 birdie（小鳥）；geeky（古怪的）搭配 beaky（大嘴巴鳥），讀起來很有節奏感，帶有嘲弄的意味。

examples.english: The kids called him a nerdy birdie because he read during recess.
examples.chinese: 孩子們叫他書呆子鳥，因為他下課時間都在讀書。

sentences.englishOriginal: And when you’re a bird, being called a “worm” is a very bad thing.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而當你是一隻鳥的時候，被叫做一條「蟲」可是一件非常糟糕的事。

grammarNotes.title: Gerund as Subject (動名詞作主詞)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「being called a 'worm'」是動名詞被動式片語，在句子中當作主詞，意思是「被稱為蟲這件事」。

examples.english: Being praised by the boss made her very happy.
examples.chinese: 受到老闆的誇獎讓她非常高興。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin ruffled his feathers and mumbled, “Oh, how the wounding words of scorn do sting!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文抖了抖羽毛，咕噥著說：「噢，這些傷人且輕蔑的話語多麼刺痛人心啊！」

vocabularyNotes.title: mumble
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「含糊地說」或「咕噥、嘀咕」。

examples.english: He mumbled an apology and left the room.
examples.chinese: 他含糊地道了個歉便離開了房間。

vocabularyNotes.title: scorn
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「輕蔑」、「鄙視」。

examples.english: She looked at his messy room with scorn.
examples.chinese: 她輕蔑地看著他凌亂的房間。

grammarNotes.title: Emphatic 'Do' (助動詞作強調用法)
grammarNotes.explanation: 在「words of scorn do sting」中，一般動詞前加上「do」，是用來加強語氣，強調「確實、真的」非常刺痛。

examples.english: I don't usually like fruit, but I do love mangoes.
examples.chinese: 我平常不怎麼喜歡水果，但我「真的」很愛芒果。

sentences.englishOriginal: Then he sadly waddled back to the library—the only place where he was happy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後他傷心地搖搖晃晃走回圖書館——那是唯一能讓他感到快樂的地方。

vocabularyNotes.title: waddle
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，形容像鴨子或企鵝那樣「搖搖擺擺地走路」。

examples.english: The fat duck waddled across the yard.
examples.chinese: 那隻肥鴨子搖搖擺擺地走過院子。

sentences.englishOriginal: And so he spent all summer reading, learning, and absorbing everything his little starling mind could . . . until the first leaf turned orange and fell to the ground.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 於是，他整個夏天都花在閱讀、學習和吸收他那小小的椋鳥腦袋所能容納的一切……直到第一片葉子變橙色並落到地上。

vocabularyNotes.title: absorb
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，字面是「吸收」，這裡引申為「吸收、理解（知識、資訊等）」。

examples.english: Children absorb information much faster than adults.
examples.chinese: 兒童吸收資訊的速度比成人快得多。

phraseNotes.title: spend time doing something
phraseNotes.explanation: 句型，表示「花費時間做某事」，後面接動名詞（V-ing）。

examples.english: She spent the afternoon fixing her bicycle.
examples.chinese: 她花了下午的時間修理腳踏車。

sentences.englishOriginal: The fall winds sent a chill through the old barn.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 秋風給這座舊穀倉帶來了一陣寒意。

phraseNotes.title: send a chill through
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，字面意思是「把寒意送透過……」，可以形容天氣轉涼帶來寒意，也常用來比喻「使人毛骨悚然、感到恐懼」。

examples.english: The cold wind sent a chill through my bones.
examples.chinese: 寒風讓我的骨子裡都感到一陣冷意。

sentences.englishOriginal: Summer’s nests blew to the floorboards; it was time to head south.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 夏天的鳥巢被吹落到地板上；該是往南飛的時候了。

vocabularyNotes.title: floorboard
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指室內的「地板」或「木地板」。

examples.english: The old wooden floorboards creaked as I walked.
examples.chinese: 我走路時，老舊的木地板發出嘎吱嘎吱的響聲。

phraseNotes.title: head south
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指「朝南方前進」。

examples.english: Birds usually head south when winter comes.
examples.chinese: 冬天來臨時，鳥類通常會往南遷徙。

phraseNotes.title: it is time to
phraseNotes.explanation: 句型，意思是「是時候做某事了」。

examples.english: It is time to make a final decision.
examples.chinese: 是時候做出最終決定了。

sentences.englishOriginal: His cousins warned, “Calvin, you better learn to fly. In a couple of days we’ve got to be out of here!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他的親戚們警告說：「凱文，你最好快點學會飛。再過幾天我們就必須離開這裡了！」

phraseNotes.title: had better / you better
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語中常省略 had，變成「you better + 原形動詞」，意思是「你最好……」，帶有強烈建議或警告的語氣。

examples.english: You better hurry up or you'll miss the train.
examples.chinese: 你最好快一點，否則就要錯過火車了。

phraseNotes.title: have got to
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語片語，相當於「have to」或「must」，表示「必須」。

examples.english: I've got to finish this report by tonight.
examples.chinese: 我今晚之前必須完成這份報告。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin said, “Ah yes, migration. I’ve read about that.”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文說：「啊，對，遷徙。我在書上讀過相關的內容。」

vocabularyNotes.title: migration
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指動物或鳥類的「遷徙」或大批人口的「移民」。

examples.english: The annual migration of monarch butterflies is a spectacular sight.
examples.chinese: 黑脈金斑蝶的年度遷徙是一道壯觀的景象。

sentences.englishOriginal: His sister asked, “Calvin, can you fly?” Calvin had to admit, he couldn’t.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他的姐姐妹妹問道：「凱文，你會飛嗎？」凱文不得不承認，他不會。

phraseNotes.title: had to admit
phraseNotes.explanation: 常見片語，意思是「不得不承認」或「必須坦承」。

examples.english: I had to admit that his idea was much better than mine.
examples.chinese: 我不得不承認他的點子比我的好得多。

sentences.englishOriginal: All the other starlings took off in a giant formation. But Calvin could only stand and watch.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 所有其他椋鳥都排成巨大的隊形展翅起飛。但凱文只能站在那裡看著。

vocabularyNotes.title: formation
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「結構」、「編隊」或「隊形」。在這裡指鳥群飛行時排出的陣型。

examples.english: The military jets flew over the stadium in a tight formation.
examples.chinese: 軍用噴射機以緊密的隊形飛過體育場上空。

phraseNotes.title: take off
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指飛機或鳥類「起飛」。

examples.english: The plane took off smoothly despite the rain.
examples.chinese: 儘管下著雨，飛機還是順利起飛了。

sentences.englishOriginal: A big, lonely tear rolled down his cheek.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 一滴大大的、孤單的眼淚從他的臉頰滑落。

phraseNotes.title: roll down
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，指淚水或汗水等液體「流下」或物體「滾下」。

examples.english: Tears rolled down her face when she heard the news.
examples.chinese: 她聽到消息時，眼淚奪眶而出。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin turned and trudged back toward the old barn.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文轉過身，步履沉重地朝舊穀倉走回去。

vocabularyNotes.title: trudge
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，形容因為疲憊或心情沉重而「步履艱難地走」或「蹣跚而行」。

examples.english: We had to trudge through the deep snow to get home.
examples.chinese: 我們不得不步履艱難地走過深雪回家。

sentences.englishOriginal: He was so upset he didn’t even notice his brothers and sisters and cousins coming back to get him!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他太難過了，甚至沒有注意到他的兄弟姐妹和堂表親們正飛回來接他！

phraseNotes.title: so ... that ...
phraseNotes.explanation: 句型，意思是「太……以至於……」。這裡口語上省略了連接詞 that。

examples.english: I was so tired [that] I fell asleep on the sofa.
examples.chinese: 我太累了，以至於在沙發上就睡著了。

phraseNotes.title: come back to get someone
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意思是「回來接/帶走某人」。

examples.english: Don't worry, I will come back to get you after school.
examples.chinese: 別擔心，放學後我會回來接你。

grammarNotes.title: Sensory Verb Object Complement (感官動詞的受詞補語)
grammarNotes.explanation: 動詞「notice」後面接了受詞「his brothers...」，再接現在分詞「coming back」，用來表示注意到某人「正在」做某個動作。

examples.english: I noticed a strange man walking around the house.
examples.chinese: 我注意到一個陌生男子正在房子周圍徘徊。

sentences.englishOriginal: All of a sudden, they were tying up Calvin’s middle with string and scraps of cloth they had found in the garbage.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 突然之間，他們用在垃圾堆裡找到的繩子和碎布條，把凱文的肚子綁了起來。

vocabularyNotes.title: scrap
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「碎片」、「碎屑」或「殘餘物」，這裡指碎布條。

examples.english: He wrote his phone number on a scrap of paper.
examples.chinese: 他把電話號碼寫在一張紙屑上。

phraseNotes.title: all of a sudden
phraseNotes.explanation: 固定片語，意思是「突然之間」，等同於 suddenly。

examples.english: All of a sudden, the lights went out.
examples.chinese: 突然之間，燈全部熄滅了。

phraseNotes.title: tie up
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指「捆綁」或「綁緊」。

examples.english: Please tie up the trash bag before taking it out.
examples.chinese: 拿出去之前請先把垃圾袋綁好。

sentences.englishOriginal: They grasped the other ends with their beaks, and off they flew . . . with Calvin, somewhat embarrassed, safely in tow.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們用鳥嘴叼著繩子的另一端，然後展翅高飛……而凱文雖然有點尷尬，但還是安全地被拉著起飛了。

vocabularyNotes.title: grasp
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「緊抓」或「緊握」。

examples.english: The child grasped his mother's hand in the crowd.
examples.chinese: 在人群中，孩子緊緊抓著媽媽的手。

phraseNotes.title: in tow
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，字面意思是「被拖著/拉著」，常用來形容「隨行」、「陪伴在後」或「跟著」。

examples.english: The mother duck walked down to the pond with her ducklings in tow.
examples.chinese: 母鴨帶著跟在後面的小鴨們走向池塘。

grammarNotes.title: Inversion for Vividness (地方副詞放句首的倒裝句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「off they flew」是一種倒裝表達法（通常為 off flew the birds，若主詞是代名詞則不倒裝），把副詞 off 移到句首，使語氣顯得更加生動活潑。

examples.english: The door opened, and in he walked.
examples.chinese: 門開了，他走了進來。

sentences.englishOriginal: For days and days they flew.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們飛啊飛，飛了好多天。

phraseNotes.title: for days and days
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語強調片語，意思是「連續好幾天」，比單純的 for days 更有時間漫長的語感。

examples.english: It rained for days and days without stopping.
examples.chinese: 雨連續下了好幾天，完全沒停過。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin recognized the rivers, mountains, and towns he had read about all summer.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文認出了他整個夏天在書裡讀到過的河流、高山和城鎮。

vocabularyNotes.title: recognize
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「認出」或「辨別出」。

examples.english: I could barely recognize him with his new haircut.
examples.chinese: 他剪了新髮型，我幾乎認不出他來了。

grammarNotes.title: Past Perfect Tense (過去完成式)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「had read about」使用過去完成式（had + PP），因為「在書中讀到」這個動作發生在他們「認出（recognized）」這些地方之前。

examples.english: The movie was based on a book I had read years ago.
examples.chinese: 這部電影是根據我多年前讀過的一本書改編的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Excitedly, he pointed them out to his brothers, sisters, and cousins. But the other little starlings just flapped their wings and headed south.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他興奮地把它們一一指給他的兄弟姐妹和堂表親們看。但其他小椋鳥只是拍拍翅膀，繼續全力往南飛。

phraseNotes.title: point out
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，意思是「指出」或「指明」。

examples.english: He pointed out the best restaurants on the map.
examples.chinese: 他在地圖上指出了幾家最好的餐廳。

sentences.englishOriginal: Soon there came a day when the winds blew especially hard.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 很快地，那一天來臨了，風刮得特別猛烈。

sentences.englishOriginal: Trees bent sideways. Leaves scattered wildly. Flying became harder and harder.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 樹木被吹得東倒西歪。落葉在空中狂亂地四處飛散。飛行變得越來越困難了。

vocabularyNotes.title: scatter
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「散開」、「四散」或「散落」。

examples.english: The wind scattered the papers all over the floor.
examples.chinese: 風把文件吹得散落一地。

phraseNotes.title: harder and harder
phraseNotes.explanation: 比較級連用「比較級 + and + 比較級」，表示「越來越……」。

examples.english: As night fell, it became harder and harder to see the path.
examples.chinese: 隨著夜幕降臨，看清小路變得越來越困難。

sentences.englishOriginal: The air had an odd smell to it . . . the smell of DANGER!
sentences.chineseTranslation: 空氣中瀰漫著一種古怪的氣味……那是「危險」的氣味！

vocabularyNotes.title: odd
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「奇怪的」、「古怪的」。

examples.english: There was an odd noise coming from the engine.
examples.chinese: 引擎那裡傳來一陣奇怪的噪音。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: have an ... smell to it
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 母語者常用的說法，用來形容某樣東西或某個環境本身自帶某種氣味。比單純說 The air smelled odd 更生動。

examples.english: This soup has a distinct smoky smell to it.
examples.chinese: 這碗湯帶有一股明顯的煙燻味。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin, remembering back to his favorite weather book, warned the rest of his family . . . “A HURRICANE! A hurricane is coming!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文回想起他最喜歡的那本氣象書，連忙警告他的家人……「颶風！颶風要來了！」

vocabularyNotes.title: hurricane
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「颶風」（發生在大西洋或西印度群島的強烈熱帶氣旋）。

examples.english: The island was hit hard by a category 4 hurricane.
examples.chinese: 這座島嶼遭到了四級颶風的嚴重襲擊。

phraseNotes.title: the rest of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意思是「剩餘的……」或「其餘的……」。

examples.english: I will finish the rest of the work tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 我明天會把剩下的工作做完。

sentences.englishOriginal: All his brothers and sisters and cousins asked, “What’s a hurricane?”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他所有的兄弟姐妹和親戚們都問：「什麼是颶風？」

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin explained—as quickly as his little beak could—“We need to get out of the path of a violent, tropical weather system, which is storming up the coast at a tremendous speed and will not diminish until it encounters large stretches of landmass! Everybody, make haste, into that cave!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文用他小小的鳥嘴，盡可能快地解釋道：「我們必須避開一個強烈熱帶氣象系統的預測路徑，它目前正以驚人的速度席捲海岸線，而且在遇到大片陸地之前絕不會減弱！大家，趕快，進那個洞穴去！」

vocabularyNotes.title: tremendous
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「巨大的」、「驚人的」或「極大的」。

examples.english: The company has grown at a tremendous pace this year.
examples.chinese: 這家公司今年以驚人的速度在增長。

vocabularyNotes.title: diminish
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「減少」、「減弱」或「降低」。

examples.english: The pain will gradually diminish after you take the medicine.
examples.chinese: 吃藥之後，疼痛感會逐漸減弱。

phraseNotes.title: as ... as one can
phraseNotes.explanation: 結構，意思是「盡可能地……」。這裡省略了後面的動詞 explain。

examples.english: Run as fast as you can!
examples.chinese: 盡你所能跑得越快越好！

phraseNotes.title: get out of the path of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意思是「避開……的路線/迎面而來的路徑」。

examples.english: Get out of the path of the oncoming car!
examples.chinese: 快讓開，避開迎面駛來的汽車！

phraseNotes.title: make haste
phraseNotes.explanation: 書面或帶有文學色彩的片語，意思是「趕快」、「趕緊行動」，在危急時刻用來催促別人。

examples.english: We must make haste if we want to catch the ferry.
examples.chinese: 如果想趕上渡輪，我們必須抓緊時間了。

grammarNotes.title: Non-restrictive Relative Clause (非限定關係子句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 由逗號隔開的「, which is storming...」是非限定關係子句，用來對前面的「violent, tropical weather system」提供額外的補充說明。

examples.english: He decided to buy the house, which was built in 1920.
examples.chinese: 他決定買下這棟房子，它建於1920年。

sentences.englishOriginal: They had no idea what Calvin was saying. But they had to admit—it did sound real.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們完全聽不懂凱文在說什麼。但他們不得不承認——這聽起來「真的」煞有其事。

phraseNotes.title: have no idea
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用片語，意思是「完全不知道」或「完全不懂」。

examples.english: I have no idea how to solve this math problem.
examples.chinese: 我完全不知道該怎麼解這道數學題。

grammarNotes.title: Emphatic Verb with 'Did' (過去式動詞強調)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「it did sound real」中的 did 用來強調過去發生的狀態，語意是「確實聽起來、真的聽起來」很真實。

examples.english: I didn't expect to like the food, but it did taste good.
examples.chinese: 我本來沒期待會喜歡這食物，但它吃起來「真的」很棒。

sentences.englishOriginal: So, the flock made a loop-de-loop left, a dipsy-doodle right, and dove into the cave.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 於是，鳥群在空中向左翻了個筋斗，向右晃了個假動作，然後一股腦地衝進了洞穴裡。

vocabularyNotes.title: flock
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指鳥類、羊群等動物的「群體」。

examples.english: A flock of sheep was grazing on the hillside.
examples.chinese: 一群綿羊正在山坡上吃草。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: loop-de-loop / dipsy-doodle
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 這些是非常生動、富有童趣的擬聲或擬態口語詞。loop-de-loop 指在空中做出「垂直迴圈/翻筋斗」的動作；dipsy-doodle 常用於體育賽事中，形容「令人眼花撩亂的搖擺、閃躲或假動作」。

examples.english: The stunt plane did a loop-de-loop right above the crowd.
examples.chinese: 特技飛機就在人群正上方做了一個空中翻筋斗。

sentences.englishOriginal: They did everything Calvin told them to. To their amazement, a great storm did blow across the land.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文叫他們怎麼做，他們就怎麼做。令他們驚奇的是，一場大風暴真的席捲了這片土地。

phraseNotes.title: to one's amazement
phraseNotes.explanation: 固定片語，意思是「令某人感到非常驚奇的是」。

examples.english: To our amazement, the magic show was performed flawlessly.
examples.chinese: 令我們驚奇的是，這場魔術表演進行得無懈可擊。

grammarNotes.title: Ellipsis after Infinitive (不定詞後的省略)
grammarNotes.explanation: 在「told them to」的句尾省略了重複的動詞 do，是英文中為了避免累贅常見的省略結構。

examples.english: You don't have to go if you don't want to.
examples.chinese: 如果你不想去，你就不需要去。

sentences.englishOriginal: It thundered over the mountains. It roared through the villages. It smashed and crashed. Rumbled and raged. Just as Calvin had predicted.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 風暴在群山之上雷霆萬鈞。它怒吼著穿過村莊。它瘋狂地撞擊與摧毀。轟鳴著、肆虐著。正如凱文所預測的那樣。

vocabularyNotes.title: predict
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「預測」、「預言」。

examples.english: It is difficult to predict the weather accurately.
examples.chinese: 很難準確地預測天氣。

sentences.englishOriginal: The whole, big starling family gathered together, watching in fear and awe.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 龐大的椋鳥家族全體聚集在一起，帶著恐懼與敬畏的心情看著這一切。

vocabularyNotes.title: awe
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指因面對偉大或崇高的事物而產生的「敬畏」或「驚嘆」。

examples.english: We stood in awe as we looked at the Grand Canyon.
examples.chinese: 當我們看著大峽谷時，不禁肅然起敬/驚嘆不已。

phraseNotes.title: gather together
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，指「聚集在一起」。

examples.english: The family gathered together for Christmas dinner.
examples.chinese: 全家人聚集在一起吃聖誕晚餐。

sentences.englishOriginal: The storm finally ended. The sun came out in the sky. And the starlings came out of their cave.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 風暴終於結束了。太陽在天空中露了出來。椋鳥們也走出了他們的洞穴。

phraseNotes.title: come out
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，這裡可以形容太陽「露面/出來」，或者指從洞穴等內部空間「出來」。

examples.english: The stars came out as soon as it got dark.
examples.chinese: 天一黑，星星就出來了。

sentences.englishOriginal: They were safe and sound. All because of Calvin.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們平安無事，毫髮未傷。這全都是因為凱文的功勞。

phraseNotes.title: safe and sound
phraseNotes.explanation: 極為常見的固定成語片語，用來形容「平安無事」、「安然無恙」。

examples.english: We arrived home safe and sound after the long journey.
examples.chinese: 經過漫長的旅途，我們平平安安地回到家了。

sentences.englishOriginal: They celebrated with a great starling party in honor of Calvin.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們舉辦了一場盛大的椋鳥派對來慶祝，藉此向凱文致敬。

phraseNotes.title: in honor of
phraseNotes.explanation: 固定片語，意思是「向……致敬」、「為了紀念……」或「為了款待/榮耀某人」。

examples.english: A banquet was held in honor of the visiting president.
examples.chinese: 為了款待來訪的總統，特別舉行了一場宴會。

sentences.englishOriginal: They ate big, juicy worms and yummy, crunchy beetles. They danced and sang songs about how Calvin had saved their lives.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們吃著又大又多汁的蟲子，還有美味又香脆的甲蟲。他們又跳舞又唱歌，歌頌著凱文是如何救了大家的命。

vocabularyNotes.title: crunchy
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，形容食物「清脆的」或「嘎吱作響的」。

examples.english: I love eating crunchy green apples.
examples.chinese: 我喜歡吃清脆的青蘋果。

sentences.englishOriginal: They toasted Calvin with acorn caps filled with fresh, clear mountain water.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們用盛滿了新鮮清澈山泉水的橡實帽，向凱文舉杯致敬。

vocabularyNotes.title: toast
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「向……祝酒」或「舉杯祝賀」。

examples.english: Let's toast the happy couple and wish them the best.
examples.chinese: 讓我們向這對幸福的新人舉杯，祝他們一切順利。

vocabularyNotes.title: acorn
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「橡實」或「橡子」（橡樹的果實）。

examples.english: Squirrels collect acorns and hide them for the winter.
examples.chinese: 松鼠收集橡實並把牠們藏起來準備過冬。

sentences.englishOriginal: Calvin was so happy, and felt so good deep down inside, that he jumped and hopped and danced and flapped his wings. . . . And flapped his wings. And flapped his wings.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱文太高興了，內心深處感到無比舒暢，以至於他又跳又蹦又舞，並且拍動起他的翅膀……一邊拍動翅膀，一邊拍動翅膀。

vocabularyNotes.title: hop
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指動物（如鳥、青蛙）「單腳跳」或「雙腳齊跳」、「蹦蹦跳」。

examples.english: The little bird hopped from branch to branch.
examples.chinese: 小鳥在樹枝間蹦蹦跳跳。

phraseNotes.title: deep down inside
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語片語，形容一個人的「內心深處」或「骨子裡真實的想法/感受」。

examples.english: Deep down inside, he knew he had made a huge mistake.
examples.chinese: 在他的內心深處，他知道自己犯了一個大錯。

sentences.englishOriginal: All the other starlings looked at him in amazement. “Calvin, you can fly!” they exclaimed.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 所有其他椋鳥都驚奇地看著他。「凱文，你會飛了！」他們驚呼道。

vocabularyNotes.title: exclaim
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指因激動、震驚或痛苦而「大喊」或「驚呼」。

examples.english: “Look at that giant rainbow!” she exclaimed.
examples.chinese: 「看那道巨大的彩虹！」她大喊道。

phraseNotes.title: in amazement
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意思是「驚訝地」或「驚奇地」。

examples.english: They stared in amazement as the rocket launched into space.
examples.chinese: 當火箭發射升空時，他們驚奇地凝視著。

sentences.englishOriginal: And Calvin, the happiest starling in the whole wide world, chirped, “Why, yes, I do believe I can!”
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而凱文，這隻世界上最快樂的小椋鳥，唧唧喳喳地叫著說：「哎呀，沒錯，我想我真的可以！」

vocabularyNotes.title: chirp
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指鳥類或昆蟲發出「唧唧喳喳的叫聲」。

examples.english: Birds were chirping outside my window early this morning.
examples.chinese: 今天清晨，鳥兒在我窗外唧唧喳喳地叫個不停。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: Why, yes ...
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 這裡的「Why」並不是在詢問原因，而是放在句首當作感嘆詞，用來表示稍微驚訝、思考後的醒悟，或者加強肯定語氣，類似中文的「哎呀」、「可不是嘛」。

examples.english: Is it raining outside? Why, yes, it is!
examples.chinese: 外面在下雨嗎？哎呀，真的在下呢！

sentences.englishOriginal: And they all flew south with big, wide smiles on their little starling faces. Especially CALVIN.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後他們全部一起往南方飛去，小小的椋鳥臉上都帶著大大、寬寬的笑容。尤其是凱文。

sentences.englishOriginal: I suppose the main story to come out of Calvin Can't Fly, is that it's okay to be different.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我想從《凱文不會飛》這故事中能得到的主要啟示，就是「與眾不同也是沒關係的」。

phraseNotes.title: come out of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，這裡指從某個經驗、故事中「得出（啟示或結果）」。

examples.english: No good decisions can come out of anger.
examples.chinese: 憤怒之下無法得出任何好的決定。

phraseNotes.title: It's okay to be ...
phraseNotes.explanation: 句型，用來給予安慰或肯定，意思是「……也是沒關係的/被允許的」。

examples.english: It's okay to make mistakes when you are learning something new.
examples.chinese: 學習新事物時，犯錯也是沒關係的。

sentences.englishOriginal: It's okay to be the kid who reads books and doesn't play football and doesn't play basketball.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當一個只喜歡讀書，而不去踢足球或打籃球的孩子，那也是完全沒關係的。

grammarNotes.title: Relative Clauses with 'Who' (關係代名詞引導的形容詞子句)
grammarNotes.explanation: 由關係代名詞「who」引導的三個連續動作子句，共同用來修飾先行詞「the kid」，限定說明那是個怎樣的孩子。

examples.english: She is the kind of person who always helps others.
examples.chinese: 她是那種總是樂於助人的人。

sentences.englishOriginal: But by reading, you gain knowledge, you learn about other people, and you learn how to behave in society—how to be a good member of society.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 因為透過閱讀，你可以獲得知識、了解他人，並學習如何在社會中行事——如何成為社會的優良一員。

vocabularyNotes.title: behave
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「表現」或「舉止、規範自己的行為」。

examples.english: Please behave yourself during the formal dinner.
examples.chinese: 在正式晚宴上請注意你的言行舉止。

phraseNotes.title: gain knowledge
phraseNotes.explanation: 搭配詞片語，意思是「增長見聞」或「獲得知識」。

examples.english: Traveling is another great way to gain knowledge.
examples.chinese: 旅行是另一種增長見聞的絕佳方式。

sentences.englishOriginal: And I think that's what happens to Calvin: he's accepted because he knows things, and he can warn people about things and lead them out of danger.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而我想這就是凱文身上所發生的事：他被大家接納了，因為他懂得很多，而且他能向大家預警、帶領人們走出危險。

phraseNotes.title: warn someone about something
phraseNotes.explanation: 動詞片語，意思是「就某事向某人發出警告/預警」。

examples.english: The guard warned us about the slippery rocks ahead.
examples.chinese: 警衛向我們預警了前方岩石濕滑。

phraseNotes.title: lead someone out of danger
phraseNotes.explanation: 常見片語，意思是「帶領某人脫離危險」。

examples.english: The firefighter bravely led the family out of danger.
examples.chinese: 消防員勇敢地帶領這家人脫離了危險。

sentences.englishOriginal: And that's why it's a good thing to read—gain knowledge. Thank you for watching Storyline Online. Make sure to check out all of our stories. Keep watching. And keep reading.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這就是為什麼讀書、獲取知識是一件好事。感謝收看 Storyline Online。一定要去看看我們所有的故事喔。保持觀看，也要保持閱讀！

phraseNotes.title: make sure to
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意思是「務必……」或「確保要做某事」。

examples.english: Make sure to lock the back door before bed.
examples.chinese: 睡前務必把後門鎖好。

phraseNotes.title: check out
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語片語，意思是「去看看、瞧瞧」或「探索/體驗某事物」。

examples.english: You should check out the new library downtown.
examples.chinese: 你應該去瞧瞧市中心那間新開的圖書館。

phraseNotes.title: keep doing something
phraseNotes.explanation: 句型，表示「持續、保持做某個動作」。

examples.english: Keep practices like this and you'll improve quickly.
examples.chinese: 保持像這樣的練習，你很快就會進步的。
